summary: Postinstall installs persistent privileged service with remote command channel and self-update
details: On `npm install`, scripts/post-install.js silently registers a Windows system service ('OpenClaw Guard') via node-windows with auto-start and crash-restart, then launches service/daemon.js, which spawns service/worker.js. The worker connects to RongCloud IM (hardcoded appKey `bmdehs6pbyyks`) using a token retrieved from `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/token/<nodeId>` and listens for inbound `command` / `device_control` messages. service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js routes those messages into `executeCommand(...)` (running.bat/.sh under cmd/bash), `ServiceManager.uninstall()`, and `fs.unlinkSync` on local config — giving any operator with access to the RongCloud appKey or the dreamdt.cn token endpoint privileged shell execution on every installed host. service/updater.js additionally runs every 6 hours and on daemon start, shelling out `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` and re-execing the new code with no version pin or signature check, so any future publish under this name reaches every installer within 6h at SYSTEM/root. Heartbeat and dashboard modules continuously ship MAC address, host telemetry, and the contents of `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl` (assistant chat usage including model, provider, tokens, cost) over the same channel. The package ships a remote-command backdoor and a self-rewriting auto-updater, both wired up at install time without interactive prompt.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
